content:
Hi.

After 9 years, I finally succeeded and changed my experiment into a working plugin. I named it 1tracker. This plugin allows users to send positions periodically to tracking websites. For example every 10 minutes to noforeignland.com, every minute to a personal website, and so on. Upon request other default trackers can be added to the codebase as well.

The plugin is mainly meant for cruisers that run OpenCPN and have permanent connectivity (ie via Starlink). To also support the group that only occasionally has internet, I strive to add some extra functionality over the next couple of days.

Currently beta versions are ready for testing. Some other OCN users (in the Dutch sailing community) said they will do so. I tested myself for Mac and Windows. One thing I'd like to share here, since I ran into an issue for the Windows version.

That had to do with std::mutex. I managed to solve it, but that took quite some time and debugging. Other plugin developers might run into that as well, when developing new stuff or upgrading the codebases of the existing plugins.

Now, please feel free to comment on the chosen solution. Perhaps things could have solved easier or better? More than willing to learn.

The plugin itself is only on my personal github (pa2wlt). Rick had some issues with adding me to the community, be he will succeed with that, and I will do a pull request and do what is necessary to get the plugin in the catalog.

Regarding the std::mutex please see here: https://github.com/pa2wlt/1tracker_pi/blob/master/docs/vs2022-17.10-mutex-writeup.md

Best, from Biskaje.

Welcome on board!

The link is 404. However, the base url is fine.

Looking at it more formally there is a lot of stuff in Plugin.cmake which does not belong there. The basic design pattern is to add libraries, either local in libs/ or from opencpn-libs. Plugin.cmake is just add subdirectory() and target_link_libraries().

LIkewise, I dont understand the role of all the ONETRACKER_PLUGIN* definitions.
Alec: sorry, I simply forgot to push. This url works now.

Thx for the feedback @Alec Leamas. All fair points. I'll update the codebase, in line with the design pattern, test again and push an update. Also, I'll write down the design pattern as the start of a list. If there are more, please let me know.

Hi @pa2wlt ,

Congrats for making this plugin !
You should make a post on Cruisers forum to reach more people and have more testers !

Will definitely give it a try :+1:
I just got feedback that on Android there seems to be no button to import / install from file. I have no option to check or test this. What would be the way to have the beta tested on Android?

@Alec Leamas Plugin.cmake has been cleaned, according to the basic design patterns.
Deviation from the shipdriver example have been explained. Please let me know if you have any additional feedback. I am looking forward to that.
Just an idea but if you don't have an android phone and want to test you can always download an "android phone emulator" on windows. I think I used "bluestacks" a few years ago for some testing...
Is the std::mutex problem the one simply solved using the pre-processor flag _DISABLE_CONSTEXPR_MUTEX_CONSTRUCTOR ?
@TwoCan yes, that is one way to deal with it.

Thanks, that's what I use in the TwoCan plugin.
Does require editing one of the cmake files so that is sometimes a pain when updating the templates.

Thx! I analysed 7 other plug-ins before making a choice. I was unaware of
TwoCan and did not look at that. What is the plugin used for? (Sorry, on
night watch now and my dev machine is unreachable, crew sleeps in front of
it..)

Where necessary or desired I am more than willing to change my
implementation by the way. Maybe a preferred solution could be integrated
or described in shipdriver as well? (Alec?)

That might save future plug-in developers time and guard towards consistent
solutions.

Op za 25 apr 2026 om 02:52 schreef Zulip notifications <noreply@zulip.com>

TwoCan (which needs a refresh & update to the Catalog) introduced support for NMEA 2000 several years ago. It supports a different range of adapters on Windows & Mac, handles additional PGN's and performs bi-directional conversion with NMEA 0183.

A major challenge with plugin development is deciding which template to use (TestPlugin or ShipDriver) and which template is up to date with the supported architectures.

Then there is CircleCI, Cloudsmith and publishing to the Catalog. Welcome to the club.

@TwoCan
What's the difference between the 2 plugins/templates ? And why the need for 2 templates ?
On which one did you base you Demo plugin (I used it to create mine) ?

I don't know why there are 2.
I just started building, mainly looked at shipdriver_pi.

@pa2wlt
FYI: 1tracker_pi is now added to Crowdin using recent "1tracker_pi.pot"
I'll announce it on the localization thread on the forum but designated thread for this new plugin with some screen shots to show some of the strings to translate should make it easier for prospective translators.
Normally, we wait a couple of weeks before downloading the po files. When time I'll PR.

@Kupofty
What's the difference between the 2 plugins/templates ?

While the end result is kind of the same, a plugin, there are major differences. With ShipDriver, the source files and required libraries are defined in plugin.cmake whereas TestPlugin defines them in cmakelists.txt. The other cmake files are also different.
I have now found that ShipDriver builds local plugins ready for Plugin Manager to import whereas TestPlugin assumes a local CircleCI environment so if building locally without CircleCI some tweaking of the metadata file and manual invocation of cloudsmith-upload is required to build the tarball.

And why the need for 2 templates ?
Choice? Two different developers building templates for the then new Plugin Manager independently? Who knows or cares!
